evals.query: I have a CreateUserRequest record in my Kora service. How do I validate that name is not blank, email matches a regex, and age is between 18 and 120?
expected_behavior: Annotates the record type with @Valid from ru.tinkoff.kora.validation.common.annotation
Uses @NotBlank on name, @Pattern("...") on email (value is the unnamed attribute), and @Range(from = 18, to = 120) on age
Notes that @Valid generates a Validator<CreateUserRequest> component in the graph
Does not use jakarta.validation / JSR-380 annotations and does not introduce a @NotNull constraint (nullability is implicit)

evals.query: How do I make a Kora service method validate its arguments before it runs?
expected_behavior: Adds @Validate to the method and annotates parameters (e.g. @Valid on a DTO param, @NotBlank/@Pattern on scalars)
Explains the aspect throws ru.tinkoff.kora.validation.common.ViolationException before the body when validation fails
States the enclosing class must be non-final (Java) / open (Kotlin) for the aspect to subclass it
Does not rely on field injection and does not invent a @Validated annotation (Kora has only @Validate)

evals.query: In my Kora DTO some fields are optional but Kora keeps rejecting them as null. How do I make a field optional?
expected_behavior: Explains every field is implicitly required and Kora generates a null check for each
Marks the optional field with a @Nullable annotation (jakarta/javax/jetbrains) in Java, or a nullable type in Kotlin
Clarifies there is no @NotNull constraint in Kora; nullability is the default and @Nullable opts out
Does not suggest jakarta.validation @NotNull or @NotNull(required = false)

evals.query: My OrderRequest contains a Customer object and a List of OrderItem. How do I get Kora to validate the nested objects and collection elements too?
expected_behavior: Marks each nested type (Customer, OrderItem) with @Valid so a Validator<T> is generated for each
Adds @Valid on the nested field/parameter (including the List) so the parent validator recurses
Explains violation paths are dotted, e.g. customer.address.city via Violation.path().full()
Does not rely on jakarta.validation cascade semantics or reflection-based traversal

evals.query: When a request fails validation in my Kora HTTP service it returns 500. How do I turn ViolationException into a structured 400 response?
expected_behavior: Uses the validation-module artifact with ValidationModule (not just validation-common)
Registers a ValidationHttpServerInterceptor tagged with @Tag(HttpServerModule.class) plus a ViolationExceptionHttpServerResponseMapper that returns HttpServerResponse.of(400, ...)
Builds the error body from exception.getViolations(), using Violation.message() and Violation.path().full()
Does not catch jakarta.validation.ConstraintViolationException or hand-roll a try/catch in each controller method instead of the interceptor

evals.query: Can Kora validation stop at the first error instead of collecting all violations?
expected_behavior: For methods, uses @Validate(failFast = true)
For manual validation, builds ValidationContext.builder().failFast(true).build() (or ValidationContext.failFast()) and passes it to validator.validate(value, context)
Explains the default (Full) mode collects all violations before throwing one ViolationException
Does not invent a validation-group mechanism or a failOnFirst property (Kora validation has no groups)

evals.query: I need a custom IBAN constraint annotation in Kora. How do I build one?
expected_behavior: Implements Validator<Iban> returning List<Violation> via context.violates(...)
Declares a ValidatorFactory<Iban> sub-interface and registers it as a @KoraApp default method returning IbanValidator::new
Creates the annotation with @Retention(RetentionPolicy.CLASS), a @Target, and @ValidatedBy(IbanValidatorFactory.class) from ru.tinkoff.kora.validation.common.annotation
Does not assume ValidatorFactory.create() can receive annotation attributes, and does not use jakarta ConstraintValidator / @Constraint

evals.query: How do I validate the return value of a Kora method, like ensuring the returned List has at least one element and each element is valid?
expected_behavior: Puts @Validate on the method to enable the aspect, @Valid on the method to validate returned objects, and @Size(min = 1) on the method to constrain the list
Explains result validation runs after the body produces the value
Mentions supported return signatures (T, Optional<T>, Mono/Flux<T> in Java; T, suspend, Flow<T> in Kotlin)
Does not invent a class-level @Validated annotation or a return-value interceptor from another framework

evals.query: How do I add Kora validation to a Kotlin data class?
expected_behavior: Annotates the data class with @Valid and uses the @field: prefix on constraints (e.g. @field:NotBlank, @field:Size)
Uses Kotlin nullable types (String?) for optional fields instead of @NotNull
Notes a data class is fine for class validation; open is only needed when the class hosts a @Validate method
Wires ksp "ru.tinkoff.kora:symbol-processors" rather than the Java annotationProcessor, and avoids jakarta.validation annotations
